SUMMARY:
One of the most significant challenges with access control is exposure risk. Exposure risk based on overly broad user access permissions can

FULL CONTENT:
![Nutanix Community Logo](https://uploads-us-west-2.insided.com/nutanix-us/attachment/19e84a9c-1afb-41af-ac9d-34a3f5d01ddb.png)
![Nutanix Community Logo](https://uploads-us-west-2.insided.com/nutanix-us/attachment/19e84a9c-1afb-41af-ac9d-34a3f5d01ddb.png)

This post was authored by Mike McGhee, Director TMM  and Rishi Thaper, Principal Product Manager

Implementing effective access control for unstructured data is a challenging and complex task. A typical mid- to large-size enterprise organization has a massive amount of unstructured data within a dynamic environment. 

Unstructured data is growing at an unprecedented pace, and to make matters worse, maintaining permissions on repositories of unstructured data is becoming even more challenging.

There can be thousands of shares in an organization, hundreds of thousands of folders and numerous control points, thousands of users and groups, and nested security groups. Every day, people change roles or leave organizations, projects & applications are initiated in different parts of organizations, requiring permission changes for various employees and contractors.

New requests for granting and revoking access come daily to IT teams as well as identity and access managers, which can make access impossible to control.

Silos between the IT and identity access management (IAM) processes further limit unified access and visibility across these groups. Active Directory (AD) security groups are modified and created regularly, and sometimes IT admins or owners change permissions on the file system’s access control lists (ACLs), adding another layer of complexity. Analyzing access risks at scale and evaluating and bubbling up the most significant security threats is a massive challenge. 

### Exposure Risk

One of the most significant challenges with access control is exposure risk. Exposure risk based on overly broad user access permissions can lead to people in an organization getting access to non-public information. The question is: How does the data, which initially had controlled access, get exposed? To answer that, you need to understand the access provisioning process.

In most cases, an IT admin will configure access on the top-level folder as per business requirements and provide access to different individuals using security groups and making changes to the ACLs. These groups may be nested, containing other security groups, and all the members get access to the data as per the permissions set in the ACL.

Complexity arises as the IAM admin, who is involved in the upkeep of access management from a directory services perspective, updates a security group to include a broad access group at a business request without knowing the repercussions of the change.

While access to other data might be a business requirement at the time, it unexpectedly opens access to this specific folder without the IAM admin having any visibility into the repercussions, causing exposure to potentially sensitive data. This can lead to significant risks from malicious insiders, disgruntled employees, hackers, and even ransomware. Global groups like everyone or authenticated users or even broad organizational groups should only have access to public data.

### Unintended Access

A second risk and challenge that organizations face is the issue of unintended access. Over time, changes to security groups may open access to sensitive information to unintended users like contractors. This can include access to a customer’s and employee’s personal data. Misconfigurations and limited visibility into who has access to the data can have grave consequences, such as data leaks or catastrophic breaches.

### Ensure Best Practices Access Provisioning

A third challenge for organizations is ensuring best practices around access provisioning and maintenance. Violations often occur, leading to more significant headaches in analyzing and remedying permissions.

One such example is broken inheritance. Generally, organizations would like to assign permissions to a top folder based on business requirements and allow propagation in the hierarchy. They can manage security effectively if the access is propagated or restricted, but it can cause issues if data becomes overly permissive in the file system hierarchy.

Here’s an example: A file system owner changes permissions to a specific folder and adds access for another group and a user. This will open up access to many more users and can be a violation of the organization’s best practices and a risky change overall.

### Excessive Access to Individuals

A fourth challenge organizations face is excessive access to individuals. Access should depend on business needs and data content. More often than not, individuals get excessive effective permissions to data due to memberships in multiple security groups and group nesting.

If their business roles require them to read and review certain data, they inadvertently get modify or full control access. Having visibility into effective access and user entitlements can ensure the timely resolution of such access issues, thus reducing risk.

### Remediation

Lastly, organizations require effective remediation in accordance with business requirements, while ensuring no disruption to operations. Once organizations get visibility into access risks, they must understand how to remediate access.

Based on an organization’s maturity, they may involve business units in managing access controls. Once business units provide inputs on permissions changes, the real work to analyze how to remediate begins.

Remediation requires determining where to make changes in the file system or security group and what-if analysis of the proposed change. Understanding remediation points for permissions is a complex but essential job for admins in any organization to reduce risk without disrupting legitimate access and business operations.

## Nutanix Data Lens Permissions Monitoring

Nutanix Data Lens has always captured audit trails, which help you understand the operations occurring in an environment, including permission changes. But as seen by the aforementioned challenges, organizations must understand more than whether a permission change occurred. They need insights into exactly what changed and whether the changes increase risk to the system.

Nutanix Data Lens permissions monitoring helps you understand the current permissions in Nutanix Files environments, the risk those permissions present, and any specific permission changes. Nutanix Data Lens also provides custom reporting across these capabilities.

### Permission Changes

With Nutanix Data Lens, you can view the full history of permission change events and examine specific events to answer questions like:

![](https://uploads-us-west-2.insided.com/nutanix-us/attachment/b223fa6b-c0ec-4a3f-a89c-a89c1c5ad4a1.png)

### Permissions Visibility

Along with seeing permission changes, you can directly view the end-to-end permissions in your environment. Nutanix Data Lens provides views and search capabilities from the share and directory perspective or from the user or group. 

From the share and directory view, you can start from the share level and drill down into folders to see the share risk score, folder owner, total size, and total file count for each entity. 

You can then choose to see which users have access to that share or folder, including the effective permissions and deeper NTFS permissions.

The users and groups page displays effective permissions from the user or group to the shares and folders on the file server. You can easily search for a specific user or group and see all the shares and folders to which they have access. You can also see Active Directory user membership for discovered groups and group membership for discovered users.

![](https://uploads-us-west-2.insided.com/nutanix-us/attachment/fad138c7-8adb-4b7d-ae7b-986cf8e91d69.png)

### Risk Score

Nutanix Data Lens further helps you understand your permissions landscape with a configurable risk score. The risk score starts with defining your risk profile. A risk profile consists of predefined risk factors such as folders with full control, modify actions, open access, without admin access, and control points (non-inherited permissions.) You define whether each risk factor has a low-, medium-, or high-risk impact. You can also define a threshold as a percentage or number of folders to measure the risk.

![](https://uploads-us-west-2.insided.com/nutanix-us/attachment/649925b8-716f-453a-b112-bb045eb8a8fc.png)

Finally, you decide which shares use the risk profile. Nutanix Data Lens calculates the risk score for each share, which is a value from 0 to 100. You can then compare the risk score between specific shares and folders to gain an initial understanding of your high-risk shares to focus on for potential remediation.

![](https://uploads-us-west-2.insided.com/nutanix-us/attachment/cdc577c6-1147-4950-b504-ff98aae67af8.png)

Nutanix Data Lens tracks changes to your risk score as permissions and group memberships are modified, creating a risk trend. The risk trend helps you understand whether risks are increasing or decreasing over time as changes occur.

![](https://uploads-us-west-2.insided.com/nutanix-us/attachment/7353b6a8-7338-4794-9e17-a573fcc11466.png)

### Permissions Dashboard

Nutanix Data Lens consolidates much of this information into a dashboard that gives you an overview of:

The Nutanix Data Lens reporting page includes templates to extract the information you see on the permissions dashboard and elsewhere. For example, you can use the reporting function to export a list of all the folders a given user or group has access to for further analysis or for sharing with other groups.

### Conclusion

With native permissions monitoring, Nutanix Data Lens helps you analyze your access risks at scale for your Nutanix Unified Storage. It helps you address the challenges of data exposure, unintended or excessive access, and broken inheritance. As file system permissions or user and group membership information change over time, the configurable risk score helps you detect unintended events.

Visibility into these events helps you limit your exposure to data leaks from malicious insiders, hackers, and ransomware attacks. These are just some of the ways that Nutanix Data Lens gives you an active defense for your unstructured data. We encourage you to visit the Nutanix Data Lens web page to learn more.

# What Are the Challenges of Data Processing Services?

![challenges of data processing services](https://perfectdataentry.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/challenges-of-data-processing-services-2.jpg)

Do you know the challenges of data processing services? There are several obstacles you may not be aware of. Maintaining data quality is one of the main challenges faced. Ensuring the data is accurate, complete, and up-to-date can be complex. Additionally, there are concerns regarding data privacy, as protecting sensitive information is crucial.

Integrating different data sources with various formats and structures can also be challenging. Storage limitations pose another obstacle, as large amounts of data need to be stored securely. Furthermore, data security risks and processing delays can hinder the efficiency of data processing services. It’s essential to be aware of the challenges of data processing services to navigate through them effectively.

## Data Quality Issues

You frequently encounter challenges of data quality issues when using data processing services. These issues can arise due to various factors, such as errors in data collection, data entry mistakes, or inconsistencies in data formatting within the context of data processing services. To ensure the [accuracy](https://perfectdataentry.com/how-can-medical-data-entry-services-improve-data-accuracy/) and reliability of the data, it is crucial to implement effective data-cleaning techniques and validation methods tailored to address the challenges of data processing services.

Data cleaning techniques involve identifying and correcting errors, inconsistencies, and inaccuracies in the data. This process includes removing duplicate records, standardizing data formats, and addressing missing or incomplete data specifically tailored to the challenges posed by data processing services. Using data cleaning techniques can improve the overall data quality and minimize the risk of misleading or erroneous results within the context of data processing services.

Data validation methods, on the other hand, focus on verifying the integrity and validity of the data within the challenges of data processing services. This involves ensuring the data meets specific predefined criteria or rules for data processing services. Data validation methods can include checks for data type, range, uniqueness, and consistency within the context of data processing services. By implementing these methods, you can identify and resolve any issues with the data, ensuring that it is accurate and reliable for [analysis](https://perfectdataentry.com/data-processing-services-assist-data-analysis/) while addressing the challenges posed by data processing services.

## Data Privacy Concerns

Challenges of data processing services pose significant concerns, especially in terms of data privacy. As a user of these services, you must be aware of the [potential risks](https://perfectdataentry.com/what-are-risks-in-outsourcing-data-entry-services/) and take necessary precautions to protect your data. One of the critical challenges is the security of your data during transmission and storage. To mitigate this risk, data encryption methods can be employed to ensure that your data remains encrypted and protected from unauthorized access. Encryption algorithms such as AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) and RSA (Rivest-Shamir-Adleman) can encrypt data at rest and in transit.

Another vital consideration amidst the challenges of data processing services is regulatory compliance. Different regions and industries have specific regulations regarding data privacy and protection. Data processing service providers must address these challenges and comply with these regulations to avoid legal and financial consequences. They should have measures in place to ensure that your data is handled under applicable laws and regulations, such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in the European Union.

## Data Integration Complexities

Regarding data integration complexities, scalability issues, and data quality assurance are two key points to consider. Scalability issues arise when integrating large volumes of data from various sources, as the process can become overwhelming and slow. Additionally, ensuring data quality throughout the integration process is crucial to avoid inaccuracies and inconsistencies that may compromise the overall integrity of the data.

### Scalability Issues in Integration

Scaling data integration poses significant complexities. As the volume of data increases, organizations face challenges in managing data transformation and processing in a scalable manner. Data transformation challenges arise when integrating data from various sources with different formats and structures. These challenges include mapping data fields, handling inconsistencies, and ensuring data quality and integrity. Additionally, data processing bottlenecks can occur when the integration process becomes overloaded with large volumes of data, leading to delays and inefficiencies. Organizations can leverage technologies such as distributed processing frameworks and parallel processing techniques to address these scalability issues. These solutions enable processing large datasets in a distributed and parallel manner, improving performance and scalability in data integration workflows.

### Data Quality Assurance

To ensure high-quality data integration, you must address the complexities of [data quality](https://perfectdataentry.com/how-can-data-cleansing-services-enhance-data-quality/) assurance. Data validation techniques and [data cleansing](https://perfectdataentry.com/what-role-does-data-cleansing-play/) methods are crucial in achieving this goal. Data validation techniques involve verifying the accuracy and consistency of data, ensuring that it meets specific requirements or standards. This can be done through various methods, such as rule-based validation or statistical analysis. On the other hand, data cleansing methods focus on identifying and correcting or removing any errors, inconsistencies, or duplicates in the data. This includes processes like data profiling, where the data is analyzed to gain insights into its structure and quality. By implementing effective data validation techniques and data cleansing methods, you can improve your integrated data’s overall quality and reliability.

## Data Storage Limitations

One of the main challenges you face when it comes to data [processing services](https://perfectdataentry.com/scaling-data-processing-services/) is data storage limitations. As the volume of data continues to grow at an unprecedented rate, it becomes increasingly challenging to store and manage all this information effectively. Data compression techniques can help mitigate this challenge by reducing the size of data files, allowing more information to be stored within the available storage capacity. However, data compression is not without its drawbacks. It can result in a loss of data quality and slower data retrieval speeds. Retrieving compressed data requires decompression, which can be time-consuming and resource-intensive

 Additionally, compressed data may not be easily searchable or accessible, posing further challenges in data retrieval. Thus, while data compression can optimize storage space, it also introduces complexities and trade-offs that must be carefully considered. Finding the right balance between data storage limitations and data retrieval challenges is crucial for ensuring efficient and effective data processing services.

## Data Security Risks

[Data security](https://perfectdataentry.com/data-processing-services-boost-data-security/) risks pose significant challenges in data processing services. Unauthorized data access and data breach threats are two critical concerns that organizations must address within these challenges. Ensuring robust [security measures](https://perfectdataentry.com/what-are-security-measures-for-medical-data-entry-services/), such as encryption protocols, access controls, and regular security audits, is crucial to safeguarding sensitive data from potential breaches.

### Unauthorized Data Access

When it comes to the challenges of data processing services, you need to be aware of the risks associated with unauthorized access to your data. Unauthorized data access poses a significant threat to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of your valuable information, thereby amplifying the [challenges faced](https://perfectdataentry.com/what-are-the-challenges-faced-in-medical-data-entry-services/) by data processing services. To mitigate this risk, it is crucial to implement robust data encryption and data access controls. Data encryption involves converting data into a code, making it unreadable to unauthorized individuals. This ensures that even if someone gains unauthorized access to your data, they cannot decipher its contents. Data access controls, on the other hand, restrict access to sensitive data based on user roles and permissions. By implementing these measures, you can protect your data from unauthorized access and minimize the potential impact of security breaches.

### Data Breach Threats

To further address the challenges of data processing services mentioned earlier, it is essential to understand the potential threats leading to data breaches in this context. One key aspect of data breach threats is inadequate data encryption methods, among the specific challenges of data processing services. Encryption plays a crucial role in protecting sensitive information from unauthorized access. Without proper encryption, data becomes vulnerable to interception and exploitation by malicious actors. Additionally, inadequate data handling protocols can contribute to data breach threats, adding to the complexities and challenges data processing services face. When data is not handled securely, it can be easily compromised, whether through accidental leaks or intentional actions. Therefore, data processing services must implement robust encryption techniques and establish stringent data handling protocols to mitigate the challenges associated with data breaches.

## Data Processing Delays

With the challenges of data processing services, you may encounter delays in processing times. These delays can significantly impact data processing efficiency and accuracy. Here are two key factors contributing to data processing delays:

**Hardware Limitations**: The performance of your hardware infrastructure, such as servers and storage devices, plays a crucial role in data processing speed. Outdated or insufficient hardware can result in slower processing times and hinder data processing efficiency.

Inadequate processing power: Insufficient CPU capacity can lead to delays in handling complex data processing tasks.

Slow storage systems: If your devices have slow read/write speeds, it can increase the time required to access and process data.

**Network Bottlenecks**: The efficiency of your network infrastructure can also impact data processing delays. Network congestion, limited bandwidth, or unreliable connections can hinder the timely data transfer between systems.

Bandwidth limitations: Insufficient network bandwidth can slow data transmission, especially when dealing with large datasets.

Latency issues: High latency can introduce delays between data requests and responses, impacting processing times.

Investing in robust hardware and optimizing your network infrastructure is crucial to mitigate data processing delays. Regular performance monitoring and maintenance can help identify and address bottlenecks to improve data processing efficiency and accuracy.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Are Data Quality Issues one of the common challenges of Data Processing Services? How can they be addressed?

Data quality issues are indeed among the everyday challenges of data processing services. You can address data quality issues in data processing services by [implementing data cleansing techniques](https://perfectdataentry.com/when-choosing-a-data-cleansing-service/) and validation strategies. These methods ensure accurate and reliable data, improving the overall quality of your processed data.

### What Common Data Privacy Concerns Arise in the Context of Data Processing Services?

Regarding data processing services, shared concerns about data privacy include data breach prevention and legal implications. It is crucial to address these challenges of data processing services to ensure the security and compliance of sensitive information.

### How Do Data Integration Complexities Impact the Efficiency and Effectiveness of Data Processing Services?

Data integration challenges can significantly impact the efficiency and effectiveness of data processing services. Complex data integration can cause delays and errors and hinder decision-making. It’s crucial to address these challenges of data processing services to optimize data processing operations.

### Are Any Limitations or Constraints Associated With Data Storage in the Context of Data Processing Services?

Data storage limitations and constraints can pose challenges in data processing services. These limitations may include capacity constraints, security considerations, and the need for efficient retrieval and analysis of stored data.

### What Are the Potential Risks and Vulnerabilities in Terms of Data Security When Using Data Processing Services?

When using data processing services, you must be aware of the potential data breach risks and vulnerabilities. It is crucial to ensure legal compliance to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access and maintain [data security](https://perfectdataentry.com/how-can-outsourcing-data-entry-enhance-security/). These risks and vulnerabilities are central to the challenges of data processing services.

Data management presents a number of challenges such as maintaining data quality, ensuring data security, integrating data from multiple sources, managing growing data volumes, and complying with regulations. These challenges affect how effectively organizations can use data for analytics and decision-making.

Organizations must navigate complexities such as data integration, quality assurance, privacy protection, and efficient data retrieval. Additionally, the need to drive actionable insights from data and strategic decision-making further adds to the intricacy of data management.

As businesses strive to harness the full potential of their data assets, it is essential to adopt robust [data management strategies](https://rivery.io/data-learning-center/data-management-strategy/) and technologies that can effectively address these complexities. This article elaborates on the common data management challenges and how to navigate the data maze successfully.

Data Quality Concerns
---------------------

Poor data quality can have a substantial impact on organizational processes, leading to inefficiencies, inaccurate analysis, and flawed decision-making. Inaccurate or incomplete data can result in misguided strategies, ineffective operations, and compromised customer experiences.

To maintain and enhance data quality standards, organizations are highly recommended to implement several strategies, such as:

*   **Establishing a robust [data governance framework](https://rivery.io/data-learning-center/data-governance-complete-guide/)** that involves data quality policies, standards, and procedures. This ensures accountability, defines data ownership, and enforces data quality controls.
*   **Validate and cleanse data** to identify and rectify inconsistencies, errors, and other data management issues. Employ automated tools and processes to streamline these activities and maintain data accuracy.
*   **Implement data integration** processes that consolidate and standardize data from disparate sources. This reduces data inconsistencies and enhances overall data quality.

Integration Challenges and How to Solve Data Management Problems
----------------------------------------------------------------

Integrating different data sources can be quite a task for businesses. These sources often hаve different formats, structures, and technologies, which makes it difficult to merge them smoothly.

Data might be stored in various systems or databases, both within and outside the organization, requiring efficient processes to extract and transform it. Now, let’s take a look at how to solve data management problems.

To streamline [data integration processes](https://rivery.io/data-learning-center/data-integration-techniques-and-strategies/), organizations can adopt practical approaches, such as:

*   developing a clear understanding of the data structure and semantics of each source
*   creating a mapping framework to align and transform data to a common format or schema.

Businesses can also use [ETL tools](https://rivery.io/data-learning-center/best-etl-tools/) that automate the extraction of data from various sources, apply necessary transformations, and load it into a target system or data warehouse. These tools streamline the integration process, reduce manual effort, and ensure data consistency.

At the same time, organizations can leverage APIs provided by data sources to establish direct connections and retrieve data in a standardized manner. APIs simplify the integration process by providing predefined methods and formats for data exchange.

**Data Silos and Fragmented Systems**
-------------------------------------

Data silos happen when different departments store data separately, which makes it difficult to view your organization’s information. This lack of interconnectedness leads to inefficiencies, as your team must manually consolidate data from different sources. As a result, this slows down decision-making.

Fragmented systems often arise due to inconsistent technology adoption across departments.

### **How to Solve**

Adopting a centralized data management system, implementing cross-departmental collaboration tools, and utilizing cloud-based data lakes can help break down these silos. Data pipeline automation tools like Rivery further increase seamless data consolidation.

Encouraging data sharing across teams and implementing a single source of truth ensures that decision-makers work with accurate and consistent information.

Data Security and Privacy
-------------------------

With the increasing reliance on data-driven technologies, data security and privacy concerns have reached new heights. Organizations and individuals alike are becoming more concerned with data management issues. At the same time, they are also more aware of the potential risks associated with data breaches, unauthorized access, and misuse of sensitive information.

### **How to Solve**

You should create strict access controls, encrypt sensitive data, and regularly conduct security audits to enhance data protection. Compliance with GDPR and CCPA should also be a focus, alongside using cloud solutions with robust security protocols.

Role-based access controls (RBAC) and multi-factor authentication (MFA) can help ensure only authorized personnel access critical data.

Scalability Issues
------------------

Scaling [data management strategies](https://rivery.io/data-learning-center/data-management-strategy/) to meet evolving business needs comes with its own challenges. Traditional data management approaches may fall short in dealing with growing data volumes, infrastructure limitations, and adapting to new technologies.

Organizations can leverage cloud-based solutions for scalable infrastructure to address this and other data management challenges. Additionally, automation and AI streamline processes can also help with scalability, while agile data governance allows for flexibility. Data virtualization is another data management strategy that can help create a unified version of data without physical movement.

### **How to Solve**

You should invest in cloud-based, scalable storage solutions. You should also optimize data processing workflows to address scalability concerns.

You should also use a modular approach to data architecture, enabling seamless scaling without major overhauls.

Lack of Data Governance
-----------------------

Lacking data governance is one of the major data management challenges that organizations face today. Data governance plays a vital role in (successfully) handling data management issues. It establishes accountability, sets data standards, and ensures compliance.

Data governance ensures adherence to regulatory requirements and industry standards related to data handling, storage, and privacy. Organizations may face compliance violations, fines, and legal consequences without proper governance.

Without effective data governance, organizations may fail to recognize and leverage the full potential of their data. Valuable insights and opportunities for innovation may go unnoticed, hindering growth and competitive advantage.

### **How to Solve**

You must develop a well-defined data governance strategy, assigning data stewards, and using governance frameworks like DAMA DMBOK can help.

You can leverage data management platforms like Rivery, which enables you to enforce governance policies effectively.

Overcoming Resistance to Change
-------------------------------

Acknowledging the human factor is crucial when addressing data management challenges. Resistance to change is a well-known human trait that can hinder the adoption of novel strategies and approaches.

In data management practices, there is a way to overcome resistance and foster a culture of adaptability through effective and clear communication, skill development and training, and giving recognition and incentives.

### **How to Solve**

You can offer comprehensive training, demonstrate the benefits of data-driven decision-making, and build a culture of innovation that can help ease transitions. Choosing user-friendly, low-code solutions can also reduce resistance.

You should focus on change management strategies, including leadership buy-in and gradual implementation, to reduce disruptions and encourage engagement.

Technological Obsolescence
--------------------------

Recognizing the impact of technological advancements is crucial for future-proofing data management systems, where organizations can:

*   embrace scalable and flexible infrastructure
*   implement robust data integration
*   leverage automation and AI
*   continuously learn

Optimizing these aspects will bring future data management issues to a minimum.

At the same time, cloud-based solutions provide scalability, while modern data integration tools support diverse sources and formats. Automation and AI streamline processes and enhance data quality. Staying up to date with emerging technologies ensures agility and the ability to leverage advancements.

### **How to Solve**

Regularly evaluating and upgrading data management tools, embracing cloud-based solutions, and integrating AI-driven analytics can help avoid technological obsolescence.

Conducting periodic technology assessments and investing in future-proof solutions ensures long-term sustainability.

Continuous Monitoring and Improvement
-------------------------------------

Ongoing assessment and optimization stand as crucial for data management architectures. Implementing a feedback system enables continuous improvement in successful data management practices. Organizations that regularly evaluate the effectiveness of their data management architecture are able to identify areas for improvement. They can also promptly address any inconsistencies and data gaps.

### **How to Solve**

You should implement monitoring dashboards, set up automated alerts for anomalies, and continuously optimize data workflows to ensure efficiency. AI-powered tools like Rivery provide proactive monitoring and maintenance.

You should also use key performance indicators (KPIs) to track data management effectiveness and drive improvement.

**Lack of Skilled Data Professionals**
--------------------------------------

Many organizations struggle with a shortage of skilled data professionals, leading to inefficiencies and slow adoption of advanced data strategies. The demand for data engineers, analysts, and scientists continues to outpace supply—a common issue throughout the industry.

### **How to Solve**

You should invest in employee training programs, work with external data consultants, and use intuitive data management platforms like Rivery to bridge the skills gap.

You can also partner with universities, offer competitive salaries, and create internal mentorship programs, which can also help attract and retain top data talent.

Key Takeaways
-------------

A robust data management architecture is paramount as it lays the foundation for efficient and effective data handling within organizations. It provides a structured framework for organizing, integrating, and governing data assets, enabling reliable and accurate insights for decision-making.

Scalability issues, data quality concerns, and the lack of data governance are some of the most common data management challenges that require immediate attention. Timely resolution of data management issues is critical to prevent potential pitfalls.

Delayed action can result in data inconsistencies, compromised data quality, regulatory non-compliance, and missed business opportunities. By addressing data management issues promptly, organizations can ensure data integrity, enhance operational efficiency, mitigate risks, and unlock the true value of their data.

Proactive handling of data management issues establishes a solid foundation for success in the data-driven era, enabling organizations to stay competitive, make informed decisions, and achieve their strategic objectives.

# 5 Common Data-Sharing Challenges and How to Overcome Them

![](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2F7p3vnbbznfiw%2F13c1kK1nrJqEN4dEhNkVv0%2F77f34464d9e9dee74aaeb058de0421af%2FMichael-Meyer.jpeg&w=128&q=85)

By Michael Meyer

Published on January 4, 2024

![Alation blog image: An illuminated cityscape intricately coded, depicting the 5 common challenges in data sharing and their solutions through strategically placed lights along the streets.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2F7p3vnbbznfiw%2F5M3q9Px7xKHQNfKkYSLt4D%2F13f5c08124d78baf0a79737b98847302%2F5-Common-Data-Sharing-Challenges-and-How-to-Overcome-Them-Featured-Blog-700x400__1_.png&w=3840&q=85)

Whether you use it to guide business-critical decisions or as part of a collaborative research project, data sharing is an essential practice for any flourishing business. Nevertheless, while its importance is undeniable, data sharing does come with its fair share of significant challenges.

There are five main concerns that businesses encounter with data sharing. We will explore what they are and the best solutions for overcoming them. But first, let’s cover the basics.

## What is data sharing?

Data sharing is the process of sharing datasets internally with authorized people, departments, or externally with other organizations — but its uses and implications vary. Take, for example, a company that uses the [best auto dialer software](https://www.convoso.com/products/autodialer-software/) for their outbound calls. This tool works even better when integrated with the company’s data-sharing infrastructure.

Agents on the phone can then be provided with real-time data on the customer they’re speaking to. Information like their purchase history, appointment schedule, or compliance information can all be made readily available in this way.

This is just one instance of data sharing, but there are many ways in which data dissemination can support [decision-making](https://www.alation.com/blog/what-is-data-driven-decision-making/), generate new ideas, and solve problems. A culture of open data sharing creates a transparent workplace where everyone works together towards common goals.

![Close-up of a laptop displaying data-sharing challenges on the screen.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2F7p3vnbbznfiw%2F1AUOmh13hDHLbOjzxtEJx0%2F9e68ca304c9cbc05bf43d455ace08b78%2Fopened-laptop-from-unsplash__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

## 5 Data sharing challenges and how to overcome them

If you’re already deeply involved with data sharing, then the chances are you’ve encountered some challenges. Read on if you’re wondering how to handle them and get the best out of your data.

### 1. Privacy concerns

You need your data to be accessible, but this availability automatically presents concerns around [customer data protection](https://www.alation.com/blog/customer-data-protection-tips/) and privacy rights. Depending on what geographic markets your company sells products and services within, you are almost certainly governed by data protection regulations like the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) in the U.S. and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in Europe.

Non-compliance with these laws can bring hefty financial penalties, not to mention irreparable damage to your company's reputation. Therefore, it is paramount that [data privacy](https://www.alation.com/blog/data-privacy-vs-data-security/) is a top priority to address in your data-sharing practices.

### Mitigating privacy concerns

#### Documentation

To create a successful foundation for data sharing, you must know and document your data. Typically, this foundational step of a data privacy program is called data mapping. It incorporates documentation of your data assets, i.e., what systems in your organization contain personal data, what kind of personal data they hold, and much more. Also, when you capture the records of processing activities (ROPA), as noted per Article 30 of GDPR, you will understand what your organization is doing with the personal data you have collected, processed, retained, and perhaps disclosed. Mapping the data will help you see any existing data-sharing risks and consider how you may reduce other risks as your organization moves forward.

#### Data Sharing Agreements (DSAs)

Ensure that your organization incorporates data processing agreements (DPAs) into your agreements when your organization shares personal data with a third party. In addition to conducting appropriate initial and ongoing due diligence about a third party, it’s vital to incorporate DPAs to ensure that all data processing aligns entirely with applicable data protection laws and regulations, given the sharing of personal data. The DPA will detail the what and why of the personal data that will be shared along with the technical and organizational measures (TOM) to protect the personal data, among other details of the relationship. These agreements are essential to the baseline set of protections in place when personal data must be shared with a third party.

#### Data minimization

Rather than indiscriminately sharing all available personal data, only share the information that is absolutely necessary for recipients to achieve their purpose. For example, if an individual does not provide food for attendees at an event, then this individual would not need to receive information about attendees’ dietary restrictions. Consider data minimization before data sharing occurs so that the organization’s risk is reduced, whether the data sharing is internal or external to your organization.

![Close-up of a mobile phone presenting data-sharing agreements, with an out-of-focus opened laptop in the background.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2F7p3vnbbznfiw%2F2dmiNTq7CdtqJdazaC8HnC%2Fe1acba784b494145f2ffd679b3495acc%2Fmobile-phone-opened-laptop__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

### 2. Security risks

As [data accessibility](https://www.alation.com/blog/accessibility-intelligence-elevated/) increases, so does the risk of unauthorized access, hacking, and insider breaches. Safeguarding sensitive information becomes requisite to avert data breaches and protect against cyber threats.

This is expected to become even more critical in the coming years, as the [future use of AI](https://www.apollotechnical.com/important-statistics-on-future-use-of-ai/) will likely bring significant advancements and higher security risks. As such, your dating-sharing framework must have iron-clad defenses against potential attacks.

### Protecting against security risks

#### Data Aggregation

Data aggregation is an excellent technique for both internal and external data sharing when the value of the data can be grouped to avoid exposing sensitive information. Take, for example, 911 calls where the caller's information must remain private. Still, the data can be aggregated by call type, date, and other categories to be highly beneficial for research.

#### Data Anonymization

Data anonymization is the process of protecting private information by scrambling the contents with different characters so that the item is no longer recognizable. When data has more value at a detailed level to be shared, organizations can use this technique, especially when sharing data externally.

#### Access controls

To successfully address security risks, use access controls liberally. They serve as a digital barrier, permitting only authorized personnel to access specific datasets. This can be achieved by establishing distinct user roles with varying access levels, employing secure authentication methods like passwords, vigilantly monitoring data access, reevaluating the need for users’ particular roles and permissions regularly, and removing or reducing roles and permissions when users’ no longer have a need to know.

#### Data-sharing platforms

Leverage your system settings extensively! Systems that enable data sharing may have many built-in access controls, including capabilities such as sharing rules, limited roles and permissions, and more. They are virtual strongholds, ensuring that only authorized personnel with a legitimate need can access and interact with relevant data. Also, stay on top of changes and updates from your third parties to maximize the value of your settings and continue limiting data sharing as appropriate. Using these capabilities effectively reduces security risks associated with data-sharing practices.

![Desktop monitor showcasing data-sharing platforms through various code snippets.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2F7p3vnbbznfiw%2F3POqouIwJtAGA5HkxwcKF9%2F5dfecd2440b6563a4e57f18f891f4fc3%2Fdesktop-with-code__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

### 3. Accountability and compliance

Adhering to [data compliance](https://www.alation.com/blog/data-compliance/) regulations is a non-negotiable responsibility for every business, but this can be a complex and constantly evolving task. Plus, you must also be able to clearly demonstrate what measures you’ve taken to abide by the rules.

With the right tools and practices, it can be easier to effectively showcase your adherence to accountability principles, potentially exposing yourself to legal and reputational risks.

### Ensuring full compliance

#### Modern data catalogs

Catalogs are equipped with advanced features like data lineage and query history, which allow organizations to carefully track the origin, movement, and usage of data throughout its entire lifecycle. For example, if you have a [cloud call center solution](https://www.convoso.com/cloud-call-center-software/) that manages data from all communication channels, you could use a modern [data catalog](https://www.alation.com/blog/what-is-a-data-catalog/) to organize all the information and offer clear insight into managing your communication data.

[Data lineage](https://www.alation.com/blog/what-is-data-lineage/)is a powerful tool that offers crystal-clear transparency into your data processes. By enabling the tracing of data back to its source, it provides a comprehensive view of the data's journey. This includes insights into the original source of the data, who accessed it, when, and how it was used. This level of detail is invaluable for demonstrating compliance with data protection regulations. For example, data lineage information can be used to identify whether specific personal data is in or out of scope for particular regulations, such as the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA). It can also show that an organization has corrected data per a user’s data subject request. Data lineage can also be very useful in the unfortunate case of a data breach. By examining data lineage, you can determine what data flows may need to be stopped or reduced to minimize further impacts of the breach.

**Query** history acts as a digital paper trail, meticulously recording every execution on the data. This includes need-to-know information, such as who initiated the query and when it occurred. By preserving this comprehensive record, you can establish an unambiguous history of data interactions. In addition, some of the modern cloud databases can also track policy control conformance, adding to better auditability.

![Close-up of a laptop on a couch, showcasing a data analytics dashboard reminiscent of Google Analytics 4.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2F7p3vnbbznfiw%2F30boXgCbzcYQaVx5QTrZyH%2F7005b65b2223ff25ad99e3b35e239c41%2Fopened-laptop-with-analytics-dashboard__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

### 4. Documentation and data context

Data is nothing without proper context and detailed documentation. Say, for example, you receive a report highlighting an increase in web traffic on a specific day, but the report doesn’t give any background information as to why.

This information is only valuable with the proper context via extra documentation. In some cases, this might include a business memo that explains the marketing campaign launched on that particular day. Data can be misinterpreted or misused without this context, undermining its value and purpose.

### Providing more context

#### Strengthen documentation practices

Enrich your data with plenty of contextual information, clearly outlining each data field's purpose and permissible use. Take, for instance, a column labeled “transaction amount” in a financial dataset. A description indicates that the data is the purchase amount and specifies it is only for billing and financial reporting purposes.

#### Accessibility

Documentation should be in a user-friendly data catalog that helps to enhance the context and is easy to navigate for all authorized users. When information is readily accessible in a format that aligns with their established workflows, it allows employees to make the most effective use of the shared data. Utilizing a data catalog contributes to the overall success of data-sharing goals.

![Home office scene: Open laptop on a desk, displaying coded snippets to highlight success in data-sharing goals.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2F7p3vnbbznfiw%2Fl5oWMNJMhZUTuBZQxsoWl%2F25fa3ecf029a04eccbddccbe75d5b5d5%2Fopened-laptop-in-neat-desk__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

### 5. Data quality assurance

Maintaining [data quality](https://www.alation.com/blog/what-is-data-quality-why-is-it-important/) throughout sharing procedures is another tricky challenge. When the standard of data is poor, it can have wide-reaching implications. Take, for instance, an [outbound power dialer](https://www.convoso.com/products/power-dialer-software/) that relies on shared contact data. If the data is inaccurate or outdated, it can result in wasted time and a poor use of resources.

Getting the most value out of your data-sharing practices relies on that data being of good quality. Confidence in your data's integrity makes it easier to make well-informed decisions and achieve data-sharing objectives.

### Preserving data quality

#### Data governance

Airtight practices that incorporate regular audits and validations uphold the highest data quality standards. Robust [data governance](https://www.alation.com/blog/what-is-data-governance/) procedures safeguard against unreliable and inaccurate data, so you can be confident that you are maintaining consistency in quality.

#### Quality checks

Keep your data in good order with stringent checks using [data quality metrics](https://www.alation.com/blog/data-quality-metrics/) and validation procedures — before sharing occurs. Scrutinizing data at the start ensures you avoid potential quality issues, so any discrepancies are addressed before they can cause any problems.

#### Data standardization

When there is inconsistency in data formatting and structuring across platforms and systems, it can cause mistakes and issues. Shared data may be subject to misunderstanding or may even be inaccessible. Ensure data format and structure are standardized to avoid errors and hold-ups.

## Make the most of your data

There are many concerns to be considered when sharing data, and if not handled correctly, they can hinder many areas of your business operations. But when you encounter the issues discussed in this article, you now know that there are multiple solutions to alleviate your woes.

Data sharing may be complex, with so many difficulties inherent around security and reliability, but if you can address these issues directly, the value you extract can be immense. Being proactive can pay huge dividends, so take measures before you find yourself up against any real problems.

When your data-sharing practices are thoroughly fortified, you can enjoy all its benefits for informed decision-making and exciting business innovation.
